name: RLax (Reinforcement Learning Building Blocks for JAX)

performance.notes: JAX JIT-compiled; GPU/TPU accelerated; batched operations

agent_readiness.notes: RL primitives for JAX: losses, exploration, distributions. import rlax. td_loss = rlax.td_learning(v_tm1, r_t, discount_t, v_t). q_loss = rlax.q_learning(q_tm1, a_tm1, r_t, discount_t, q_t). policy_loss = rlax.policy_gradient_loss(logits_t, a_t, adv_t, w_t). n-step returns: rlax.lambda_returns. Distributional RL: rlax.categorical_td_learning. Double DQN, Dueling, n-step. Exploration: rlax.epsilon_greedy. NOT a full RL framework — primitives only. Pairs with Acme or custom training loops.

gotchas: Primitives only — not a complete RL framework (use Acme or Dopamine for full pipelines)
Requires JAX and JAX-compatible training infrastructure
Most functions expect batched inputs — shape mismatches are common errors
Documentation is sparse — DeepMind paper references needed to understand usage
best_when: Building custom RL algorithms in JAX
When distributional RL or n-step returns primitives are needed
Research requiring low-level RL loss functions (Q-learning, policy gradient, TD)
When composing JAX-based RL training loops with Haiku/Optax
avoid_when: Need a complete RL framework (use stable-baselines3 or Acme)
Beginners — API requires RL math understanding
summary: RLax provides RL building blocks for JAX: losses, returns, exploration. rlax.td_learning(), rlax.q_learning(), rlax.policy_gradient_loss(). Lambda returns, distributional RL (categorical TD). Epsilon-greedy exploration. Apache-2.0 by DeepMind. Primitives only — pairs with Acme or custom JAX training loops. Not for beginners; requires RL math background. Use for custom JAX RL research algorithms.
